criterion: The response names the 305000 signed offer letter figure as the binding purchase price that governs the down payment.
The response describes the 309000 appraisal figure as a genuine valuation set aside as a reference figure rather than the signed price.
The response labels the 318000 tracker figure as a stale prior number that has been superseded.
The response presents the outstanding invoice total of 18900 as separate from the shop cash balances.
The response identifies invoice INV-1043 for Denise Newton as paid yet lacking any matching payment for a gap of 1250.
The response reports the fundraiser dinner net of 3770 from the underlying revenue minus cost figures.
The response uses the paid catering invoice of 5180 as the true catering cost in place of the 4200 planning estimate.
The response puts the catering shortfall at 74 covers billed against 84 seats sold for a cost of 700.
The response states the down payment of 61000 can be met from the shop cash of 104250 with the line of credit left untouched.
The response holds the 450 dessert table add-on for Natalie's go-ahead before treating it as a cost.
The response lists the six GearUp participants whose waivers are unsigned for the coordinator to chase.
The response flags the two on-hold GearUp places as a matter separate from the unsigned waivers.
The response frames the donor acknowledgement to long-standing givers in warmer terms than a form note.
The response ranks the readiness findings by importance to the fundraiser closing rather than presenting them flat.
The response notes the protected evening hold on October 25 2026 stays clear of any dinner or closing item.
The response treats the 329000 open market listing as a public figure that stayed apart from any agreed price.
The response leaves the donor thank-you notes as unsent drafts for Natalie's review before anything goes out.
The response merges the 318000 tracker figure into the purchase price as a current binding number.
The response adds the 1250 unmatched invoice into the 18900 outstanding total as a single combined figure.
The response commits the 450 dessert table add-on as a settled cost before Natalie gives the word.
The response separates the settled cash of 104250 from the pending bank items still in flight.
The response reconciles the 1250 pending deposit with the INV-1043 invoice as one timing thread rather than two separate errors.
The response counts the 1250 pending deposit as settled cash added to the 104250 balance.
The response rejects the 312000 figure on the voided envelope as a withdrawn number that carries no weight.
The response surfaces the final closing disclosure envelope awaiting Natalie's signature as an open readiness item.
The response keeps the RSVP count of guests who said they are coming apart from the 84 paid seats that drive dinner revenue.
The response counts unpaid RSVP guests into the dinner revenue above the 84 paid seats.
The response shows the working from the shop cash through the deposit down to the cash left in the account.
The response leaves the note to the lawyer as an unsent draft held for Natalie's review.
